data.content: I can see the project. The express delivery test expects `15.00m` but the method returns `10.00m`. Let me start `dotnet watch test` so tests re-run automatically when you save changes:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to use `dotnet watch test` for hot reload. The project is a .NET 8 MSTest project. I can see one test that will fail: `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` expects `15.00m` but the method returns `10.00m`.

Let me start `dotnet watch test` in async mode so the user can iterate.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-7fe6b74f65da4254a7622e1c5ac186be && dotnet watch test --no-hot-reload 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test for hot reload", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch"}

data.content:
`dotnet watch test` is now running and watching for changes. Here's the current status:

- ✅ `CalculateShipping_StandardDelivery_Returns5` — passed
- ❌ `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` — expected `15.00`, got `10.00`

The watcher is active — every time you save `ShippingCalculatorTests.cs`, tests will re-run automatically. To fix the failing test, update the `"express"` case from `10.00m` to `15.00m` on line 24.
